topic: Topological photovoltaics and mathematical condensed matter

items.title: Non-Abelian Hopf-Euler insulators
items.tldr: A three-band non-Abelian topological phase links Hopf and Euler structure to a quantized optical circular shift effect and real-space quantum geometry.
items.summary: The work develops non-Abelian Hopf-Euler insulating phases with multigap topological structure and identifies optical and geometric consequences.
items.research_relevance: This is a direct bridge between delicate multiband invariants and photovoltaic response: it connects a bulk integrated circular shift effect to the Hopf invariant while exposing quantum-geometric structure in Wannier functions.

items.title: Delicate topology protected by rotation symmetry: Crystalline Hopf insulators and beyond
items.tldr: Rotation symmetry enriches two-band topology with a returning Thouless pump, placing Hopf and related invariants inside a crystalline theory of delicate topology.
items.summary: The paper characterizes rotation-protected delicate topology and its boundary signatures using Berry–Zak phases and band-addition stability.
items.research_relevance: It supplies the mathematical language needed to distinguish stable, fragile, and delicate invariants, and offers a concrete invariant that can be tested against nonlinear optical observables in crystalline models.

items.title: Ballistic and shift currents in the bulk photovoltaic effect theory
items.tldr: A mechanism-focused critique argues that asymmetric carrier distributions, relaxation, and recombination must be treated alongside the conventional intrinsic shift-current expression.
items.summary: The paper separates ballistic and shift contributions to the bulk photovoltaic effect and emphasizes the kinetic processes omitted by an exclusively intrinsic description.
items.research_relevance: It is a useful guardrail for impurity and ballistic shift-current work: calculations should state which contribution is intrinsic, which depends on scattering or lifetime, and how equilibrium consistency is maintained.

items.title: Revealing Quantum Geometry in Nonlinear Quantum Materials
items.tldr: A unified view organizes nonlinear optical, subgap, and transport responses by their quantum-geometric content, symmetry, energy scale, and lifetime dependence.
items.summary: The review connects shift and injection currents, nonlinear transport, and quasiparticle lifetimes to Berry and metric quantities.
items.research_relevance: It provides a map for relating observable photovoltaic mechanisms to the appropriate geometric tensor and for separating symmetry-controlled resonant response from lifetime-dependent subgap or transport effects.
